Question
========
Which of the following correctly expresses $7.2$ in scientific notation?

Answerlist
----------
* $7.2 \times 10^{0}$
* $7.2 \times 10^{1}$
* $0.72 \times 10^{1}$
* $72 \times 10^{-1}$
* $7.2 \times 10^{-1}$

Solution
========
Since 7.2 is already in the correct range (between 1 and 10), we do not need to shift the decimal point at all. This means the exponent must be 0:
$$7.2 = 7.2 \times 10^{0}$$

Remember that $10^{0} = 1$, so $7.2 \times 10^{0} = 7.2 \times 1 = 7.2$.

Meta-information
================
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exname: sci_note_zero_power
exshuffle: TRUE
